content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings AAA and BBB, both of length NNN and containing only lowercase English letters.

You also have an integer KKK.

You can perform the following operation:


Choose a continuous substring of AAA of size exactly KKK, and a character ccc.

Then, replace all characters in the substring with ccc.

Formally, you choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+11 \leq i \leq N-K+11‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+1) and a character ccc, and then set all of Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,Ai+K‚àí1A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_{i+K-1}Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai+K‚àí1‚Äã to ccc.


For example, if A=antearA = \texttt{antear}A=antear and K=2K = 2K=2,


If we choose i=2i = 2i=2 along with the character `p`, AAA will turn into appear\texttt{appear}appear.
If we instead i=5i = 5i=5 and the character `r`, AAA will become anterr\texttt{anterr}anterr.
Check whether it's possible to make AAA equal to BBB through a series of such transformations. If it is possible, also output a list of transformations of size at most 2‚ãÖN2 \cdot N2‚ãÖN.

It can be proven that if it is possible to make AAA equal to BBB, it is also possible in 2‚ãÖN2 \cdot N2‚ãÖN transformations.

If multiple answers are possible, any of them will be accepted.

Note that you do **not** need to minimize the size of the list of transformations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and KKK - the size of the strings and the size of the substrings to choose.
The second line of each test case contains AAA - a string of size NNN.
The third line of each test case contains BBB - a string of size NNN.




Output Format
For each test case:



If it is possible to transform AAA to BBB, first output M(0‚â§M‚â§2‚ãÖN)M (0 \le M \le 2 \cdot N)M(0‚â§M‚â§2‚ãÖN) - the number of transformations you would like to perform.

Then output MMM lines, each containing an integer iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+11 \le i \le N - K + 11‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+1) and a character ccc, indicating that you want to change the substring S[i,i+K‚àí1]S[i, i + K - 1]S[i,i+K‚àí1] to character ccc.
If it is impossible, output a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.

If multiple solutions exist, you may print any of them.

Note that you do **not** have to minimize MMM, as long as M‚â§2NM \leq 2NM‚â§2N.
constraints: AAA and BBB both contain lowercase English letters only.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=BA = BA=B already holds.
Test case 222: It can be proven that it's impossible to transform AAA into BBB.
Test case 333: Choose the substring A[1,4]A[1, 4]A[1,4] of size exactly K=4K = 4K=4 and replace with c to get A=ccccA = \texttt{cccc}A=cccc.
Now A=BA = BA=B.
